classification_axis: Primary observable mechanism used by the trigger rule to generate the alert anchor; this is independent of ATT&CK semantics and evidence condition.

families.name: Process and command execution
families.definition: Rules triggered primarily by process creation, parent-child relationships, executable identity, or command-line features.

patterns.name: Command-line behaviour match
patterns.definition: A process or audited command matches security-relevant program, option, target, or redirection features.

patterns.name: Script-interpreter behaviour
patterns.definition: A script interpreter is identified through encoded, hidden, obfuscated, discovery, download, or module-loading command features.

patterns.name: Execution-chain relationship
patterns.definition: The alert depends on a parent-child or staged execution relationship rather than one isolated command token.

patterns.name: Executable name or path anomaly
patterns.definition: The executable name, extension, Unicode presentation, or launch path is the primary observable alert surface.

families.name: Registry and policy modification
families.definition: Rules triggered by registry writes that change persistence, logon, credential, or security-control configuration.

patterns.name: Registry persistence value
patterns.definition: A registry value associated with logon or autorun behaviour is created or modified.

patterns.name: Security-control registry change
patterns.definition: A registry value controlling security telemetry or defensive service behaviour is modified.

families.name: Persistence object creation
families.definition: Rules triggered by creation or modification of scheduled jobs, services, or WMI persistence objects.

patterns.name: Scheduled-job definition
patterns.definition: A cron or Windows scheduled-task definition is created, edited, or configured for later execution.

patterns.name: Service configuration
patterns.definition: A service definition or binary path is created or changed in a persistence-relevant way.

patterns.name: WMI subscription object
patterns.definition: A WMI command-line consumer or subscription object contains a persistence-relevant command payload.

families.name: Cross-process and sensitive-process access
families.definition: Rules triggered by process-handle access, remote thread creation, or runtime attachment to another process.

patterns.name: Sensitive-process handle access
patterns.definition: A process opens a high-access handle to a sensitive credential-bearing process.

patterns.name: Cross-process thread injection
patterns.definition: A remote thread is created in another process with an anomalous or unbacked start address.

patterns.name: Runtime instrumentation attachment
patterns.definition: A privileged instrumentation process attaches to another running process through an operating-system tracing primitive.

families.name: Authentication and session anomaly
families.definition: Aggregate rules triggered by repeated connection or authentication events within a declared time window.

patterns.name: Inbound connection burst
patterns.definition: Repeated inbound connections from one source to an authentication service exceed a declared threshold.

patterns.name: Failed-authentication spray
patterns.definition: Rapid failures across multiple target identities satisfy count, time-window, and distinct-user thresholds.

families.name: Network and remote-resource activity
families.definition: Rules triggered by endpoint network connections or access to remote management and shared resources.

patterns.name: Endpoint network connection
patterns.definition: A process-level network event matches protocol, direction, endpoint, or management-port features.

patterns.name: Remote resource access
patterns.definition: A file-share, named-pipe, or policy-resource access event matches a security-relevant remote object pattern.

families.name: Module loading
families.definition: Rules triggered by one or more library image-load events involving a security-relevant host process or path.

patterns.name: Process module-load pattern
patterns.definition: A host process loads one or more libraries from a path or burst pattern that forms the alert surface.

families.name: Web and HTTP request activity
families.definition: Rules triggered by HTTP request paths, user agents, parameters, response status, or web-endpoint command features.

patterns.name: HTTP request pattern
patterns.definition: An HTTP request matches repository, scanner, method, path, user-agent, or response-status features.

patterns.name: Web endpoint command parameter
patterns.definition: A request to an uploaded server-side endpoint carries an encoded or command-bearing parameter.

families.name: System and monitoring state
families.definition: Rules triggered by explicit operating-system state or monitoring-health notifications rather than process semantics.

patterns.name: Host state anomaly
patterns.definition: A native operating-system event reports an anomalous shutdown or availability state.

patterns.name: Monitoring health change
patterns.definition: A monitoring service reports loss of endpoint connectivity or visibility.
